title: Multipart Upload State, Parts, Checksums, and Cleanup · Go Semantics Case: Multipart Threshold Part Size
summary: A worker starts multipart uploads without durable state, chooses tiny parts, loses ETags, retries concurrent parts blindly, and never aborts abandoned uploads. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the multipart threshold part size increment in recoverable multipart-upload state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose single versus multipart transfer and part size from object size, part-count limits, memory, network, retry, and completion constraints.
Bound aggregate part concurrency, buffers, descriptors, NICs, DNS behavior, progress callbacks, and caller admission across simultaneous transfers.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Multipart Threshold Part Size · Theory Multipart Threshold Part Size
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 5990 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate multipart part state, retained ETag evidence, cancellation, and abort cleanup; cap bytes, time, attempts, concurrency, and spend before starting work; then introduce a duplicate event or ambiguous response and reconcile without duplicate effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose single versus multipart transfer and part size from object size, part-count limits, memory, network, retry, and completion constraints.
